question.text: Vikram's AIF scheme has invested in 8 companies. One company's promoter wants to sell his 60% stake. The AIF, holding 15%, has tag along rights. What can the AIF do?

answer.text: Nothing — the promoter can sell freely
Exercise tag along rights to sell its 15% stake on the same terms as the promoter's sale
Demand the promoter sell to the AIF instead

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tag along rights allow the AIF (minority holder at 15%) to join the promoter's sale on the same terms and conditions. If the buyer is willing to pay the promoter a certain price, the AIF can sell its 15% at that same price, ensuring fair treatment. **The Trap:** Tag along doesn't block the sale — it allows participation. The AIF can choose to exercise or not exercise its tag along right depending on whether the sale terms are attractive. **Cross-Exam Context:** Practical application of tag along rights is tested in XIX-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Tag along rights are valuable in real-world scenarios. Without them, a promoter could sell at a premium while leaving the AIF trapped in the company with a new controlling shareholder who may have different priorities.
